context: Eating out is one of the best parts of a trip, and you can do the whole thing in Spanish: ask for the menu, order, and pay. You'll be seated and served at the table, so a little politeness goes a long way.
explanation: Start by asking for "el menu" (the menu), then order with "quiero" (I would like) plus the dish, and "por favor". When you're done, ask for "la cuenta" (the bill) - it won't arrive until you do.

examples.note: In Spain the bill comes only when you ask for it.
